hypothesis: Gradient-descent optimization of crystal structures using differentiable XRD-similarity losses (negative cosine similarity, MSE, negative entropy) cannot reliably recover ground-truth structures from realistically distorted lattices and fractional coordinates (nontrivial σl and σc) because the XRD-based loss landscape is highly nonconvex with many local minima, whereas symmetry-constrained optimization or energy-based relaxation with CHGNet substantially improves recovery.
